title: Java News Roundup: Value Objects, WildFly 41, TornadoVM, LangChain4j, Oracle AI Agent Studio
gist: A weekly Java ecosystem roundup notable for the reintroduction of Value Objects under Project Valhalla, TornadoVM adding FP8 support for AI workloads, LangChain4j and Oracle pushing agentic AI patterns into Java, and Open Liberty patching eight CVEs.

insights.claim: JEP 401, Value Objects (Preview) under Project Valhalla, has been reintroduced to Candidate status after originating as a 2020 draft, proposing objects with only final fields, no identity, distinguished solely by their field values.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Value objects are a long-awaited JVM performance feature; progress signals Valhalla nearing usable maturity after years of delay.

insights.claim: TornadoVM 5.1.0 added support for E4M3 and E5M2 eight-bit floating-point storage in its CUDA backend, plus an opt-in staged host-to-device transfer path for PTX and CUDA backends.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: FP8 support brings Java's GPU-acceleration framework in line with AI hardware trends, extending Java's reach into ML compute.

insights.claim: LangChain4j 1.18.0 introduced a Belief-Desire-Intention (BDI) agentic pattern to formalize psychological concepts for software/robotic systems, plus TextToSpeech (OpenAI) and Mistral Batch API support.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Agentic patterns and multi-provider model support are landing in the Java AI ecosystem, keeping Java relevant for enterprise LLM apps.
insights.quote: provides a structured method to formalize psychological concepts for software and robotic systems

insights.claim: Oracle introduced an AI-native builder for Oracle AI Agent Studio for Fusion Applications, unifying no-code, low-code, and pro-code development into one Fusion-native framework, though both are subscription services.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Oracle embedding agentic app-building into Fusion signals enterprise-suite vendors racing to monetize agentic AI via subscriptions.

insights.claim: WildFly 41 shipped a JDK 25 edition of its container, S2I builder and runtime images that replace the JDK 17 edition, plus bootable JARs, signaling migration momentum toward newer JDK baselines.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Enterprise app servers moving default images from JDK 17 to JDK 25 indicates the LTS upgrade cycle is accelerating.

insights.claim: Open Liberty 26.0.0.7 addressed eight CVEs causing denial-of-service, HTTP request smuggling and server-side request forgery, and now tracks logged-out SSO cookies by default so they can't be replayed after logout.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Eight CVEs in one release is a reminder of ongoing security debt in enterprise Java runtimes; patch cadence matters for exposure.

insights.claim: Quarkus introduced Quarkus Shim, an experimental extension that adds, wraps, or replaces Java class behavior at build-time augmentation with no Java agent or runtime instrumentation, working in JVM, dev, and native modes.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Build-time class transformation without runtime agents fits Quarkus's native-compilation performance thesis.
insights.quote: There is no Java agent and no runtime instrumentation.
